title: Polycystic Kidney Disease
chief_complaint: I found out I have high blood pressure at a health screening. My father died of kidney failure at 50 and was on dialysis. I feel fine but I'm worried about my kidneys.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Bilateral flank masses. Large, palpable kidneys. No tenderness.
physical_exam.Skin: No rashes. No angiofibromas (rules out TSC).

hidden_data.renal_us: Bilateral massively enlarged kidneys (Right 22cm, Left 20cm). Innumerable cysts throughout both kidneys. Loss of corticomedullary differentiation. Liver cysts also present.
hidden_data.cmp: Creatinine 1.8, BUN 25, eGFR 42 (CKD Stage 3b). Normal electrolytes.
hidden_data.genetic: PKD1 mutation (autosomal dominant). Family history confirms ADPKD.
hidden_data.mra_brain: Screening MRA: 3mm anterior communicating artery aneurysm (low rupture risk).

correct_diagnosis: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease (ADPKD) - PKD1 Mutation with CKD Stage 3b, Hypertension, Hepatic Cysts, and Small Cerebral Aneurysm
key_learning_points: ADPKD = most common inherited kidney disease. PKD1 (85%, more severe) or PKD2 (15%, milder). Affects 1 in 500-1000

Clinical: HTN (often first sign), bilateral enlarged cystic kidneys, CKD progressing to ESRD by age 50-60 (PKD1). Extrarenal: Liver cysts (80%), cerebral aneurysms (8%), mitral valve prolapse, diverticulosis
Diagnosis: US showing ≥3 cysts per kidney (age 15-39), ≥2 per kidney (age 40-59). Genetic testing confirms
Treatment: Tolvaptan (vasopressin antagonist) slows cyst growth and eGFR decline. Strict BP control <110/75 (ACEi/ARB). Treat UTIs aggressively (infected cysts = long antibiotic course)
Screening: MRA brain for aneurysm in patients with family history of SAH. If aneurysm >7mm or posterior circulation: neurosurgical referral
